title: Getting started with lbstproj
text: tutorial walks complete lbstproj workflow using fake oncology project. use trial dataset — simulated randomized clinical trial 200 patients assigned Drug Drug B — demonstrate : Scaffold standardized project Register outputs Table Tables (TOT) Write run data preparation, figure, table scripts Generate self-contained report

title: Getting started with lbstproj
text: create_project() builds full directory structure adds DESCRIPTION file blank Table Tables. pass force = TRUE open = FALSE skip interactive prompts. project now following structure:
code: library(lbstproj)  create_project(   path = tmp_proj_dir,   title = "Fake oncology trial",   client = "John Doe",   department = "Oncology",   author = "Jane Doe",   email = "jane.doe@university.com",   open = FALSE,   force = TRUE ) fs::dir_tree(tmp_proj_dir, recurse = TRUE) #> /tmp/RtmpLp9SSY/fake-trial #> ├── DESCRIPTION #> ├── R #> │   ├── data #> │   ├── figures #> │   ├── functions #> │   └── tables #> ├── data #> │   ├── figures #> │   ├── processed #> │   ├── raw #> │   ├── tables #> │   └── tot #> │       └── table_of_tables.xlsx #> ├── docs #> │   ├── costing.docx #> │   └── meeting_notes.docx #> ├── fake-trial.Rproj #> ├── report #> │   └── utils #> └── results #>     ├── figures #>     ├── reports #>     └── tables

title: Getting started with lbstproj
text: trial dataset bundled lbstproj CSV. Copy data/raw/: dataset 200 patients eight variables: treatment arm (trt), age, biomarker level (marker), tumour stage (stage), histological grade (grade), binary response, survival endpoints (death, ttdeath).
code: # In a real project you would place your raw data file in data/raw/ manually. fs::file_copy("path/to/trial.csv", "data/raw/trial.csv") trial_raw <- read.csv("data/raw/trial.csv") head(trial_raw) #>      trt age marker stage grade response death ttdeath #> 1 Drug A  23  0.160    T1    II        0     0   24.00 #> 2 Drug B   9  1.107    T2     I        1     0   24.00 #> 3 Drug A  31  0.277    T1    II        0     0   24.00 #> 4 Drug A  NA  2.067    T3   III        1     1   17.64 #> 5 Drug A  51  2.767    T4   III        1     1   16.43 #> 6 Drug B  39  0.613    T4     I        0     1   15.64

title: Getting started with lbstproj
text: Table Tables (TOT) central registry every output project produce. lives data/tot/table_of_tables.xlsx four columns: project plan two figures two tables: filling spreadsheet, call import_tot() validate cache .rds file: can always inspect TOT load_tot():
code: tot_data <- data.frame(   id = c("fig-01", "fig-02", "tab-01", "tab-02"),   type = c("figure", "figure", "table", "table"),   name = c("fig-age-dist", "fig-response", "tab-baseline", "tab-response"),   caption = c(     "Age distribution by treatment arm.",     "Tumour response rate by treatment arm.",     "Baseline patient characteristics by treatment arm.",     "Tumour response rates by treatment arm."   ),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) tot_data #>       id   type         name                                            caption #> 1 fig-01 figure fig-age-dist                 Age distribution by treatment arm. #> 2 fig-02 figure fig-response             Tumour response rate by treatment arm. #> 3 tab-01  table tab-baseline Baseline patient characteristics by treatment arm. #> 4 tab-02  table tab-response            Tumour response rates by treatment arm. import_tot() #> ℹ Importing Table of Tables (TOT) to data/tot/tot.rds load_tot() #> # A tibble: 4 × 4 #>   id     type   name         caption                                            #>   <chr>  <chr>  <chr>        <chr>                                              #> 1 fig-01 figure fig-age-dist Age distribution by treatment arm.                 #> 2 fig-02 figure fig-response Tumour response rate by treatment arm.             #> 3 tab-01 table  tab-baseline Baseline patient characteristics by treatment arm. #> 4 tab-02 table  tab-response Tumour response rates by treatment arm.

title: Getting started with lbstproj
text: create_from_tot() reads TOT generates stub R scripts entry. Always dry run first preview created: satisfied, run without dry_run create stubs: stub pre-filled header, get_info() retrieve TOT metadata, library calls, placeholder comments: fill placeholder sections analysis. next sections show completed scripts oncology project.
code: create_from_tot(dry_run = TRUE) #>  #> ── Figures ── #>  #> ℹ Figures: 2 in TOT, 0 on disk, 0 matched. #> ℹ Figures: would generate 2 missing programs. #> → info #> • R/figures/fig-age-dist.R #> • R/figures/fig-response.R #>  #> ── Tables ── #>  #> ℹ Tables: 2 in TOT, 0 on disk, 0 matched. #> ℹ Tables: would generate 2 missing programs. #> → info #> • R/tables/tab-baseline.R #> • R/tables/tab-response.R create_from_tot(dry_run = FALSE) #>  #> ── Figures ── #>  #> ℹ Figures: 2 in TOT, 0 on disk, 0 matched. #> ℹ Figures: generating 2 missing programs. #> → success #> • R/figures/fig-age-dist.R #> • R/figures/fig-response.R #> ✔ Fig-Age-Dist file created at R/figures/fig-age-dist.R. #> ✔ Fig-Response file created at R/figures/fig-response.R. #>  #> ── Tables ── #>  #> ℹ Tables: 2 in TOT, 0 on disk, 0 matched. #> ℹ Tables: generating 2 missing programs. #> → success #> • R/tables/tab-baseline.R #> • R/tables/tab-response.R #> ✔ Tab-Baseline file created at R/tables/tab-baseline.R. #> ✔ Tab-Response file created at R/tables/tab-response.R. #' Name         : fig-age-dist.R #' Author       : Jane Doe #' Date         : 10 Mar 2026 #' Purpose      : description #' Files created: #'  - `results/figures/fig-age-dist.png/pdf` #' Edits        : #'  - 10 Mar 2026: Created file.  # File info ---------------------------------------------------------------  info <- get_info(fig-01)  # Packages ----------------------------------------------------------------  library(dplyr) library(ggplot2)  # Load data ---------------------------------------------------------------  data <- NULL # Load processed data here  # Generate figure ---------------------------------------------------------  fig <- NULL # Replace NULL with your figure code, e.g. ggplot(data) + ...  # Save figure -------------------------------------------------------------  lbstproj::export_figure(fig, name = "fig-age-dist", ext = "png")

title: Getting started with lbstproj
text: cleaning reshaping happens R/data/. script loads raw CSV, coerces categorical variables factors, saves result save_data(). running script can inspect processed dataset:

title: Getting started with lbstproj
text: Age distribution treatment arm.
code: # R/figures/fig-age-dist.R  library(dplyr) library(ggplot2)  info <- get_info("fig-01") trial <- readRDS("data/processed/trial-clean.rds")  fig <- ggplot(trial, aes(x = trt, y = age, fill = trt)) +   geom_violin(alpha = 0.4, trim = FALSE) +   geom_boxplot(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.8, outlier.shape = NA) +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +   labs(x = "Treatment arm", y = "Age (years)") +   theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +   theme(legend.position = "none")  save_figure(fig, name = "fig-age-dist", width = 5, height = 4) #> Warning: Removed 11 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range #> (`stat_ydensity()`). #> Warning: Removed 11 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range #> (`stat_boxplot()`).

title: Getting started with lbstproj
text: Tumour response rate treatment arm.
code: # R/figures/fig-response.R  library(dplyr) library(ggplot2)  info <- get_info("fig-02") trial <- readRDS("data/processed/trial-clean.rds")  response_rate <- trial |>   group_by(trt) |>   summarise(rate = mean(response, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, .groups = "drop")  fig <- ggplot(response_rate, aes(x = trt, y = rate, fill = trt)) +   geom_col(width = 0.5, alpha = 0.8) +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +   scale_y_continuous(     limits = c(0, 100),     labels = scales::label_percent(scale = 1)   ) +   labs(x = "Treatment arm", y = "Response rate (%)") +   theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +   theme(legend.position = "none")  save_figure(fig, name = "fig-response", width = 5, height = 4)

title: Getting started with lbstproj
text: Tables saved gt objects optionally exported Word. workflow uses gtsummary produce publication-ready summaries, converts gt as_gt() saving.

title: Getting started with lbstproj
code: # R/tables/tab-baseline.R  library(dplyr) library(gtsummary)  info <- get_info("tab-01") trial <- readRDS("data/processed/trial-clean.rds")  tab <- trial |>   select(trt, age, stage, grade) |>   tbl_summary(     by = trt,     label = list(       age ~ "Age (years)",       stage ~ "Tumour stage",       grade ~ "Grade"     )   ) |>   add_overall() |>   add_p() |>   modify_header(label ~ "**Characteristic**") |>   bold_labels() |>   as_gt() |>   gt::tab_header(title = info$caption)  save_table(tab, name = "tab-baseline", export = FALSE) readRDS("data/tables/tab-baseline.rds")

title: Getting started with lbstproj
code: # R/tables/tab-response.R  library(dplyr) library(gtsummary)  info <- get_info("tab-02") trial <- readRDS("data/processed/trial-clean.rds")  tab <- trial |>   select(trt, response) |>   mutate(     response = factor(response, levels = c(1, 0), labels = c("Yes", "No"))   ) |>   tbl_summary(     by = trt,     label = list(response ~ "Tumour response")   ) |>   add_p() |>   modify_header(label ~ "**Variable**") |>   bold_labels() |>   as_gt() |>   gt::tab_header(title = info$caption)  save_table(tab, name = "tab-response", export = FALSE) readRDS("data/tables/tab-response.rds")

title: Getting started with lbstproj
text: scripts written, run_all_figures() run_all_tables() source every script R/figures/ R/tables/ alphabetical order. also cross-check file list TOT warn discrepancies.
code: run_all_figures() run_all_tables()

title: Getting started with lbstproj
text: create_report() reads TOT generates Quarto file report/report.qmd code chunk every figure table, order appear TOT. generated file looks like (excerpt):
code: create_report(output_type = "html") #> ✔ Writing report to report/report.qmd. #> ℹ Use `run_report()` to render the report. --- title: "Fake oncology trial" subtitle: "John Doe (Oncology)" author: "Jane Doe" date: "10 March 2026" format:   html:     embed-resources: true     toc: true     toc-location: left-body     number-sections: true execute:   echo: false   warning: false   message: false ---  ```{r pkg} library(here) # To generate relative file paths library(gt) # Needed to display gt tables in Word  # Create list of captions tot <- readRDS("tot.rds") caption_list <- setNames(as.list(tot$caption), tot$name)  # Define function to add caption to tables add_caption <- gt::tab_header ```     ```{r fig-fig-age-dist } #| fig-cap: !expr 'caption_list[["fig-age-dist"]]'  knitr::include_graphics(here("results/figures/fig-age-dist.png")) ```  {{< pagebreak >}}

title: Getting started with lbstproj
text: Call run_report() render Quarto file HTML (DOCX): reviewing HTML report, archive results/reports/ date-stamped filename using archive_report():
code: run_report() archive_report()

title: Getting started with lbstproj
text: full lbstproj workflow glance:
code: library(lbstproj)  # 1 — Scaffold the project create_project(path = ".", title = "My project", author = "Jane Doe")  # 2 — Fill in data/tot/table_of_tables.xlsx, then import it import_tot()  # 3 — Generate script stubs for every TOT entry create_from_tot(dry_run = FALSE)  # 4 — Fill in the scripts, then run them all run_all_files("data") run_all_figures() run_all_tables()  # 5 — Build and render the report create_report(output_type = "html") run_report() archive_report()

title: Authors and Citation
text: Alexandre Bohyn. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Bohyn (2026). lbstproj: Tools Create Use Standardized R Projects L-Biostat Group. R package version 0.1.0, https://l-biostat.github.io/lbstproj/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {lbstproj: Tools To Create And Use Standardized R Projects For The L-Biostat Group},   author = {Alexandre Bohyn},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://l-biostat.github.io/lbstproj/}, }

title: Copilot Instructions for lbstproj
text: lbstproj R package L-Biostat group initializes manages standardized statistical analysis projects. creates project scaffolding, manages “Table Tables” (TOT) metadata tracking system, provides save/load helpers outputs, renders reports.

title: Copilot Instructions for lbstproj
code: # Install dependencies devtools::install_deps()  # Run all tests devtools::test()  # Run a single test file testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-save.R")  # Run a single named test testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-save.R", filter = "save_figure")  # Check the package (includes tests, examples, vignettes) devtools::check()  # Rebuild documentation from roxygen2 devtools::document()  # Build pkgdown site locally pkgdown::build_site()

title: Copilot Instructions for lbstproj
text: package organized around project lifecycle: Initialize — create_project() scaffolds new project directory fixed folder structure, DESCRIPTION file, table_of_tables.xlsx (TOT). Create scripts — create_file() generates R scripts Mustache templates inst/templates/. create_from_tot() auto-generates scripts entries TOT. Save outputs — save_figure(), save_table(), save_data() write outputs standardized paths (output/figures/, output/tables/, data/processed/). Run scripts — run_all(), run_all_figures(), run_all_tables() execute scripts relevant R/ subdirectory. Report — create_report() generates Quarto report template; create_chunk() inserts TOT-linked code chunks; run_report() renders . Table Tables (TOT): Excel file (data/tot/table_of_tables.xlsx) central metadata registry. tracks every figure table id, type, name, caption. TOT loaded/validated via tot.R queried via get_info.R. Test helpers helper-project.R create minimal -memory TOT fixtures. Template rendering: inst/templates/ contains Mustache templates (.R .qmd). rendered using whisker package variables like {{name}}, {{author}}, {{date}}, {{caption}}, {{path}}. Project root detection: Functions rely usethis/rprojroot conventions — .Rproj file DESCRIPTION project root signals project boundary.

title: Copilot Instructions for lbstproj
text: Every R source file begins ASCII art header block:
code: # ├────────────┐ # │  lbstproj  │ # └────────────┘ # filename.R: Short description of what this file contains

title: Copilot Instructions for lbstproj
text: Roxygen2 Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE) — docs use markdown. Inline formatting uses cli-style markup: .arg arguments, .val values, .file paths, .fn function names, .strong emphasis. Every exported function @param, @return, @examples.

title: Copilot Instructions for lbstproj
text: Use cli::cli_abort() errors context-rich messages. Use cli::cli_alert_success(), cli::cli_alert_info(), cli::cli_alert_warning() user feedback. Respect quiet parameter (package option lbstproj.quiet) suppress output.

title: Copilot Instructions for lbstproj
text: Tests use testthat edition 3. Test helpers (tests/testthat/helper-*.R) define local_*() functions create isolated temporary projects clean via withr::local_dir() usethis::local_project(). Use local_lbstproj_project(with_tot = TRUE) get full temp project TOT fixture. Tests create temporary file system state tempdir() — never write package source tree tests.

title: Copilot Instructions for lbstproj
text: functions: snake_case. Parameters follow conventions surrounding codebase (path, name, id, overwrite, quiet, open). Internal (non-exported) helpers documented @keywords internal kept file primary exported function utils.R / utils_path.R / checks.R.

what: Adding new file types or templates
title: Copilot Instructions for lbstproj
text: Add new template inst/templates/ using Mustache syntax. Add new type create_file() dispatch document . Add corresponding run_all_<type>() wrapper run_all.R scripts type bulk-runnable.

title: lbstproj
text: lbstproj provides lightweight set tools L-Biostat group create, maintain, use standardized statistical analysis projects R. enforces consistent folder structure, keeps every figure table registered central Table Tables (TOT), makes easy generate reproducible Quarto reports.

title: lbstproj
text: Currently package available CRAN, needs installed github.
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("L-Biostat/lbstproj")

title: lbstproj
code: library(lbstproj)  # 1 — Scaffold a new project create_project(path = ".", title = "My project", author = "Jane Doe")  # 2 — Fill in data/tot/table_of_tables.xlsx, then import it import_tot()  # 3 — Generate R script stubs for every TOT entry create_from_tot(dry_run = FALSE)  # 4 — Fill in the scripts, then run them all run_all_files("data") run_all_figures() run_all_tables()  # 5 — Assemble and render the report create_report(output_type = "html") run_report() archive_report()

title: lbstproj
text: create_project() produces following layout:
code: my-project/ ├── data/ │   ├── raw/          # Raw input files (never modified) │   ├── processed/    # Cleaned datasets (.rds) │   ├── tables/       # Saved gt tables (.rds) │   ├── figures/      # Saved ggplot objects (.rds) │   └── tot/          # table_of_tables.xlsx + cached tot.rds ├── R/ │   ├── data/         # Data preparation scripts │   ├── figures/      # Figure scripts │   ├── tables/       # Table scripts │   └── functions/    # Shared helper functions ├── results/ │   ├── figures/      # Final PNG / PDF figures │   ├── tables/       # Word tables (.docx) │   └── reports/      # Archived reports ├── report/           # Quarto report └── docs/             # Meeting notes, costing documents

title: lbstproj
text: See Get Started vignette full walkthrough using simulated oncology trial dataset.

title: Archive a rendered report — archive_report
text: Moves rendered report report/ directory results/reports/ directory renames include current date project version. function typically used rendering report run_report(), save copy rendered output.

title: Archive a rendered report — archive_report
code: archive_report(   file = "report.html",   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = getOption("lbstproj.quiet", FALSE) )

title: Archive a rendered report — archive_report
text: file Character. name report file archive. Default: "report.html", since default file name report generation process. overwrite Logical. Indicate whether overwrite existing archive file. Default: FALSE, error raised archive file already exists. quiet Logical. TRUE, suppress informational messages. Important messages (e.g. directory creation errors) still shown. Default: FALSE unless global option lbstproj.quiet set otherwise.

what: Check if a file can be overwritten — check_can_overwrite
title: Check if a file can be overwritten — check_can_overwrite
text: Check file can overwritten

title: Check if a file can be overwritten — check_can_overwrite
code: check_can_overwrite(path, overwrite, what = "File")

title: Check if a file can be overwritten — check_can_overwrite
text: path Character. Path file check overwrite Logical. Whether file can overwritten . Character. type file checked. used customize error message file overwritten. Default: "File". generic description

title: Check if a file can be overwritten — check_can_overwrite
text: Invisibly return path file

what: CLI report for create_programs() sync step — cli_report_program
title: CLI report for create_programs() sync step — cli_report_program
text: CLI report create_programs() sync step

title: CLI report for create_programs() sync step — cli_report_program
code: cli_report_program(   type,   n_tot,   n_disk,   matched = character(),   new = character(),   extra = character(),   dry_run = FALSE )

title: CLI report for create_programs() sync step — cli_report_program
text: type One "tables" "figures". n_tot Number programs declared TOT. n_disk Number .R files found disk. matched Character vector filenames TOT disk. new Character vector filenames create. extra Character vector filenames disk TOT. dry_run Logical.

title: CLI report for create_programs() sync step — cli_report_program
text: Invisibly returns list counts.

what: Create a quarto code chunk from the TOT — create_chunk
title: Create a quarto code chunk from the TOT — create_chunk
text: Generate quarto code chunk include figure table report based information stored table tables (TOT). element can identified id name. chunk includes correct path element well caption quarto-safe chunk label (starting tbl tables fig figures allow cross-referencing). function called Rstudio console, code chunk always copied clipboard easily paste report file needed.

title: Create a quarto code chunk from the TOT — create_chunk
code: create_chunk(   output_type = "html",   id = NULL,   name = NULL,   print = TRUE,   pad = TRUE )

title: Create a quarto code chunk from the TOT — create_chunk
text: output_type Character. Output format use building code chunk. match output type report. "docx" "html" supported. Default: "html" id Character. Identifier element TOT. Either name must provided . name Character. Name element TOT. Either id must provided . print Logical. TRUE, print generated chunk console. Default: TRUE allow quick visual check. pad Logical. TRUE, add blank lines chunk insert page break . Default: TRUE

title: Create a quarto code chunk from the TOT — create_chunk
text: rendered quarto chunk character string, invisibly

what: Create a DESCRIPTION file for the project — create_description
title: Create a DESCRIPTION file for the project — create_description
text: create_description() creates DESCRIPTION file project, using project root directory "package name" adding fields: Title, Author, Client, Department. function check arguments validity. assumes checked create_project() function.

title: Create a DESCRIPTION file for the project — create_description
code: create_description(title, client, department, author, version, quiet = FALSE)

what: Create an R file from a template — create_file
title: Create an R file from a template — create_file
text: Create R file template. Dedicated templates used type "data", "figure", "table". type uses default template. Files created R/<type>(s)/ (e.g. R/figures/ type = "figure"), except type = "data" goes R/data/, type = "analysis" goes R/analysis/. target directory exist, created automatically.

title: Create an R file from a template — create_file
code: create_file(   type,   name,   open = TRUE,   quiet = getOption("lbstproj.quiet", FALSE),   ... )

title: Create an R file from a template — create_file
text: type Character. Used choose subdirectory template. name Character. File name (without .R). open Logical. Open file creation. quiet Logical. TRUE, suppress informational messages. Important messages (e.g. directory creation errors) still shown. Default: TRUE unless global option lbstproj.quiet set otherwise. ... Additional fields passed data file template.

title: Create an R file from a template — create_file
text: Invisibly returns path file created.

title: Create an R file from a template — create_file
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Hypothetical project layout: # . # └─ R/ #    ├─ figures/ #    └─ tables/  # 1) Create a figure file in `R/figures/` create_file(type = "figure", name = "hr_by_age") # > ✓ Figure file created at 'R/figures/hr_by_age.R'  # 2) Create a table file in `R/tables/` create_file(type = "table", name = "baseline_characteristics.R") # > ✓ Table file created at 'R/tables/baseline_characteristics.R'  # 3) Create an analysis file (uses default template) in R/analysis/ # > ℹ Created directory 'R/analysis'. # > ✓ Analysis file created at 'R/analysis/primary_model.R'.  # 4) Calling again does not overwrite create_file(type = "analysis", name = "primary_model") # > ! File 'R/analysis/primary_model.R' already exists and will not be overwritten. } # }

code: create_from_tot(dry_run = TRUE, quiet = getOption("lbstproj.quiet", FALSE))

title: Create all R scripts listed in the table of tables (TOT) — create_from_tot
text: dry_run Logical. TRUE, files created function reports generated. Default: TRUE avoid generate wrong scripts mistake. quiet Logical. TRUE, suppress informational messages. Default: FALSE unless global option lbstproj.quiet set otherwise. default option can changed

title: Create all R scripts listed in the table of tables (TOT) — create_from_tot
text: Invisibly returns NULL. function called side effects (file creation CLI reporting).

title: Create all R scripts listed in the table of tables (TOT) — create_from_tot
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Hypothetical project structure: # R/ # ├── figures/ # │   └── fig_01_flowchart.R # └── tables/ #     └── tab_01_baseline.R  # TOT contains: #   - fig_01_flowchart #   - fig_02_primary #   - tab_01_baseline #   - tab_02_primary  # Dry run (no files created) create_from_tot()  # Example CLI output: # # Figures # ℹ Figures: 2 in TOT, 1 on disk, 1 matched. # ℹ Figures: would generate 1 missing program (keeping 1 existing). # ℹ Figures: would create 1 program in R/figures. # • R/figures/fig_02_primary.R # # Tables # ℹ Tables: 2 in TOT, 1 on disk, 1 matched. # ℹ Tables: would generate 1 missing program (keeping 1 existing). # ℹ Tables: would create 1 program in R/tables. # • R/tables/tab_02_primary.R  # Actual creation create_from_tot(dry_run = FALSE) } # }

title: Create a New Project — create_project
text: create_project() initializes new project standardized structure specified path. prompts user essential metadata provided. function creates necessary directories, DESCRIPTION file, table tables (TOT) Excel file. project successfully created, opens new project RStudio.

title: Create a New Project — create_project
code: create_project(   path = ".",   title = NULL,   client = NULL,   department = NULL,   author = NULL,   email = NULL,   version = "1.0.0",   open = rlang::is_interactive(),   force = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Create a New Project — create_project
text: path Path project created (default current directory). title Title project. client Name client. department Department code name. author Author name (first last). email Author email. version Project version (default 1.0.0). open TRUE, opens new project RStudio (default TRUE interactive session). force TRUE, skips confirmation prompt creating project (default FALSE). quiet TRUE, suppresses informational messages project creation (default FALSE).

title: Create a New Project — create_project
text: Invisibly returns active project path.

title: Create a New Project — create_project
text: following directory structure created inside project:
code: ├── data │   ├── figures │   ├── processed │   ├── raw │   ├── tables │   └── tot ├── docs ├── results │   ├── figures │   ├── tables │   └── reports ├── R │   ├── data │   ├── figures │   ├── functions │   └── tables └── report     └── utils

title: Create a New Project — create_project
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ create_project(   path = ".", # uses current directory   title = "Example Project",   client = "Client Name",   department = "DEP",   author = "Jane Doe",   version = "0.1.0" ) } # }

what: Create a Quarto report from the TOT — create_report
title: Create a Quarto report from the TOT — create_report
text: Create Quarto report combining report template figures tables listed table tables (TOT), order appear. Captions labels taken TOT, updates TOT reflected automatically generated report. report written report/report.qmd.

title: Create a Quarto report from the TOT — create_report
code: create_report(output_type = "html", quiet = getOption("lbstproj.quiet", FALSE))

title: Create a Quarto report from the TOT — create_report
text: output_type Character. Output format report template. Must one "html" "docx". Default: "html". quiet Logical. TRUE, suppress informational messages. Important messages (e.g. directory creation errors) still shown. Default: FALSE unless global option lbstproj.quiet set otherwise.

title: Create a Quarto report from the TOT — create_report
text: Invisibly returns path generated report file.

what: Create the standard project structure — create_structure
title: Create the standard project structure — create_structure
text: create_structure() creates standard folder structure project. folders already exist skipped.

title: Create the standard project structure — create_structure

title: Create a Empty Table of Tables (TOT) Excel file in the project — create_tot

text: "data/tot/" directory exist, created. TOT file already exists target directory, overwritten.

title: Ensure a directory exists — ensure_dir_exists
text: Create directory (recursively) exist already throw error needed. Paths must relative active project, absolute.

title: Ensure a directory exists — ensure_dir_exists
code: ensure_dir_exists(path, create = TRUE)

title: Ensure a directory exists — ensure_dir_exists
text: path Character. Directory path. Must relative active project absolute. create Logical. FALSE, throws error directory exist. TRUE creates . Default: TRUE

title: Ensure a directory exists — ensure_dir_exists
text: Invisibly returns normalized path directory, relative active project.

title: Ensure a file does not exist — ensure_file_does_not_exist
text: Invisibly returns normalized path file, relative active project.

what: Get information from the Table of Tables (TOT) — get_info
title: Get information from the Table of Tables (TOT) — get_info

code: get_info(id = NULL, name = NULL)

text: id Character single TOT identifier (character numeric). Provide either .arg id .arg name, . name Character. single TOT name (character). Provide either .arg name .arg id, .

text: named list containing columns matched TOT row.

what: Import the Table of Tables (TOT) — import_tot
title: Import the Table of Tables (TOT) — import_tot
text: Reads TOT Excel file data/tot/table_of_tables.xlsx, validates structure, saves RDS file data/tot/tot.rds.

title: Import the Table of Tables (TOT) — import_tot
code: import_tot(quiet = FALSE)

title: Import the Table of Tables (TOT) — import_tot
text: quiet Logical. TRUE, suppress informational messages. Default: FALSE unless global option lbstproj.quiet set otherwise. default option can changed using options(lbstproj.quiet = TRUE)

title: Import the Table of Tables (TOT) — import_tot
text: Invisibly returns TOT data tibble.

title: Package-wide options for lbstproj — lbstproj-options
text: lbstproj exposes one global option controls whether informational messages printed package functions.

title: Package-wide options for lbstproj — lbstproj-options
text: lbstproj.quiet (logical, default FALSE) — TRUE, suppresses informational messages (e.g. "Saved …", "Importing TOT …") across every function package. Individual functions also accept quiet argument overrides option single call.

title: Package-wide options for lbstproj — lbstproj-options
text: Silence messages entire session:   Restore default (verbose) behaviour:   Override option single call:
code: options(lbstproj.quiet = TRUE) options(lbstproj.quiet = FALSE) save_figure(fig, name = "my-fig", quiet = TRUE)   # silent save_figure(fig, name = "my-fig", quiet = FALSE)  # verbose

what: lbstproj: Tools To Create And Use Standardized R Projects For The L-Biostat Group — lbstproj-package
title: lbstproj: Tools To Create And Use Standardized R Projects For The L-Biostat Group — lbstproj-package
text: package provides functions easily start new statistical analysis project R. creates standardized folder structure files, allow easier collaboration reproducible research. also provides functions easily load preprocess data, generate export figures tables, create reports various formats.

title: lbstproj: Tools To Create And Use Standardized R Projects For The L-Biostat Group — lbstproj-package
text: Maintainer: Alexandre Bohyn alexandre.bohyn@kuleuven.(ORCID)

what: Load the Table of Tables (TOT) — load_tot
title: Load the Table of Tables (TOT) — load_tot
text: Simple wrapper function load TOT RDS file.

title: Load the Table of Tables (TOT) — load_tot
code: load_tot(refresh = TRUE)

title: Load the Table of Tables (TOT) — load_tot
text: refresh Logical; TRUE, re-imports TOT Excel file using import_tot(). Default TRUE TOT may updated frequently.

title: Load the Table of Tables (TOT) — load_tot
text: tibble containing TOT data following columns: id: Unique identifier item. type: Type item (either "table" "figure"). name: Name item. caption: Caption item.

title: Load the Table of Tables (TOT) — load_tot
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{  tot <- load_tot() } # }

what: Run R Scripts in a Project Subdirectory — run_all_files
title: Run R Scripts in a Project Subdirectory — run_all_files
text: run_all_files() sources R scripts matching glob pattern inside subdirectory project's R/ folder. target subdirectory derived type argument using convention create_file(): "figure" maps R/figures/, "table" maps R/tables/, "data" maps R/data/, "analysis" maps R/analysis/, value maps R/<type>s/. type values "figure" "table", function additionally checks discrepancies files directory table tables (ToT) sourcing, issues warning found. run_all_figures() run_all_tables() convenient wrappers around run_all_files().

title: Run R Scripts in a Project Subdirectory — run_all_files
code: run_all_files(type, glob = "*.R", quiet = getOption("lbstproj.quiet", FALSE))  run_all_figures(glob = "*.R", quiet = getOption("lbstproj.quiet", FALSE))  run_all_tables(glob = "*.R", quiet = getOption("lbstproj.quiet", FALSE))

title: Run R Scripts in a Project Subdirectory — run_all_files
text: type Character. type scripts run. Controls subdirectory R/ searched: "figure" → R/figures/ "table" → R/tables/ "data" → R/data/ "analysis" → R/analysis/ value → R/<type>s/ glob Character. glob pattern passed fs::dir_ls() include exclude files. Default: "*.R" — matches R scripts (case-sensitive). quiet Logical. TRUE, suppress informational messages. Default: FALSE unless global option lbstproj.quiet set otherwise. default can changed via options(lbstproj.quiet = TRUE).

title: Run R Scripts in a Project Subdirectory — run_all_files
text: Invisibly returns character vector sourced file paths.

title: Run R Scripts in a Project Subdirectory — run_all_files
text: run_all_figures(): Run R scripts R/figures run_all_tables(): Run R scripts R/tables

title: Run R Scripts in a Project Subdirectory — run_all_files
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ run_all_files("figure") run_all_figures()                                 # equivalent to the above run_all_files("data") run_all_files("figure", glob = "fig-[0-9]*.R")   # only numbered figures run_all_files("figure", quiet = TRUE)             # suppress all messages } # }

title: Render a quarto report — run_report
text: Renders quarto (.qmd) report file located report/ directory. rendered output saved report/ directory name (different extension). report files render file name. file already exists, overwritten without warning. want keep copy rendered report, use archive_report() function rendering move results/reports/ directory unique name.

title: Render a quarto report — run_report
code: run_report(   file = "report.qmd",   quiet = getOption("lbstproj.quiet", FALSE),   ... )

title: Render a quarto report — run_report
text: file Character. name report file render. file found, function throws error. Default: "report.qmd", default name produced create_report() quiet Logical. TRUE, suppress informational messages. Important messages (e.g. directory creation errors) still shown. Default: FALSE unless global option lbstproj.quiet set otherwise. ... Additional arguments passed rendering function, case, quarto::quarto_render().

title: Render a quarto report — run_report
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Default usage run_report() # Rendering a specific file to a specific name run_report(file = "new_report.qmd", output_file = "final_report_FINAL_v3.0") } # }

what: Save outputs in the project — save_outputs
title: Save outputs in the project — save_outputs
text: functions standardize outputs saved lbstproj project: save_data() saves data frame rds data/processed. save_table() saves table rds data/tables optionally export docx results/tables. save_figure() saves figure png (another extension) results/figures. save another R object (e.g. model, mice object, ...) file, need use base::saveRDS manually define file path. function saves object using given name, validating . Names can contain letters, numbers, hyphens.

title: Save outputs in the project — save_outputs
code: save_data(   data,   name,   overwrite = TRUE,   quiet = getOption("lbstproj.quiet", FALSE) )  save_table(   table,   name,   export = TRUE,   overwrite = TRUE,   quiet = getOption("lbstproj.quiet", FALSE),   ... )  save_figure(   figure,   name,   extension = "png",   width = 6,   height = 4,   overwrite = TRUE,   quiet = getOption("lbstproj.quiet", FALSE),   ... )

title: Save outputs in the project — save_outputs
text: data data.frame save. name Character. File name without extension. overwrite Logical. TRUE, overwrite existing file name. FALSE, error thrown target file already exists. Default: TRUE. Outputs overwritten default. quiet Logical. TRUE, suppress informational messages. Default: FALSE unless global option lbstproj.quiet set otherwise. default option can changed using options(lbstproj.quiet = TRUE) table gt_tbl object save. export Logical. TRUE, also export table .docx file. Default: TRUE. Word tables always produced unless specified otherwise. ... Additional arguments passed saving function: save_table(): passed gt::gtsave save_figure(): passed ggplot2::ggsave figure ggplot object save. extension Character. Output format figures: "png" "pdf". Default: "png" width, height Numeric. Width height saved figure inches. Default: width 6 height 4. fits nicely portrait A4 page.

title: Save outputs in the project — save_outputs
text: Invisibly returns path saved file.

code: type_to_subdir(type)

text: type Character. file type (e.g. "figure", "table", "data", "analysis").

title: Map a file type to its R subdirectory — type_to_subdir
text: Character. subdirectory name.

text: tot_data data frame (tibble) validate.

title: Validate the structure of a TOT data frame — validate_tot
